title: Argument Labels
description: Write readable functions by using both internal parameter names and external argument labels.
problemStatement:
Readable code! Write a function `greet(person:from:)` that takes two string parameters. When called, it should print e.g., `"Hello Alice from London!"`.

**Requirement:** The function should be called as `greet(person: "Alice", from: "London")`.
hints: Argument labels make Swift code read like an English sentence.
